english: 2. mči-ma blág-pa (mchi ma blag pa) to shed tears. — 3. in blág-pa méd-pa (blag pa med pa), the free translation of आश्रवकायिक Burn. I, 309 takes it in the signification given by Sch. to bde-blág (bde blag), and explains it by 'bare of every convenience or comfort'.
to chew, secondary form to ldád-pa (ldad pa) Lex.

pf. blus (blus), to buy off, to ransom, to redeem, mi de blú-ru ɣtóṅ-ba (mi de blu ru gtong ba) to pay in order to redeem a man, to pay as a ransom for him Glr.; p'ug-ron-gyi srog blus (phug ron gyi srog blus) he redeemed the life of the dove Dzl.; ˚di-dag-gis rgyal-poi mgo blu-o ('di dag gis rgyal po'i mgo blu'o) therewith I will redeem the king's head Dzl.; to recover, to redeem, ɣté-ba (gte ba), a pawn, pledge, security C.; blu-rín (blu rin) the money or price paid for the redeeming of persons or goods, ransom.

molten image; blúgs-pár (blugs par) casting-mould; blúgs-ma (blugs ma) cast metal, statues, relieves (cf. ˚búr-ba ('bur ba)); blugs-ɣzár (blugs gzar), dgáṅ-blugs (dgang blugs) v. ɣzar (gzar); jà-blugs (ja blugs) urn-shaped vessel for pouring out tea etc.; spyi-blugs (spyi blugs) v. spyi-bo (spyi bo) sub spyi (spyi); már-blugs (mar blugs) oil-pitcher.
1. vb. ldud-pa (ldud pa). - 2. sbst. to blú-ba (blu ba), release, ransom, redemption Sch. - blúd-bu (blud bu) v. rlúd-bu (rlud bu).

dull, stupid; stupidity, foolishness; `blún-po (blun po) stupid, foolish; fool, idiot; blún-po la-lá ... ˚dzin (blun po la la ... 'dzin) some fools consider it...; blún-poi lugs (blun po'i lugs) foolery, fool's opinion, fool's wisdom, expressions frq. used in scientific works to defeat antagonistic views; dgé-ba mi byéd-pai mi ni blún-po yin (dge ba mi byed pa'i mi ni blun po yin) the man without virtue is a fool; ˚dód-yón-la čags šiṅ-tu blun ('dod yon la chags shing tu blun) to be given to lust is folly Pth.; byol-soṅ-p'yúgs-pas blun (byol song phyugs pas blun) more stupid than a beast Mil.; blún-ɣtam (blun gtam), blún-tsig (blun tshig) foolish talk, foolery; blún-dad (blun dad) superstition Mil. (cf. dṅos-dad (dngos dad)).
v. blú-ba (blu ba); blús-ma (blus ma) ransom Cs.

I. rarely bló-ba (blo ba) mind (Was. 314 बोधि) 1. the intellectual power in man, understanding, mkás-pai blo daṅ ldán-pa (mkhas pa'i blo dang ldan pa) Dzl., blo rno-ba (blo rno ba) Glr. talented, gifted; blo čén-po (blo chen po) (čúṅ-ṅu (chung ngu)) of great (small) mental abilities C.; blo ɣsál-te (blo gsal te) of a clear understanding, sharp-witted Dzl.; šes-pai blo (shes pa'i blo) sagacity, intelligence, judgment Dzl.; blo-rgyá (blo rgya) Sch. comprehensive intellectual power; blo myur-žiṅ (blo myur zhing) being of quick comprehension, sharp Dzl.; blo-ráb (blo rab), -˚bríṅ (-'bring), -dmán-pa (-dman pa) of sound, moderate, weak intellects or mental faculties Mṅg., the last expression is frq. used in modestly speaking of one's self Glr. and elsewb.; bló-yi mún-pa (blo yi mun pa) intellectual darkness, a darkened mind Glr.; blo-bág (blo bag) narrow-minded, weak in intellect Sch.; k'yod ni blo nór-ro (khyod ni blo nor ro) you are mistaken; blos-lčógs-pa (blos lcogs pa) 'to be competent in mind or judgment' Sch.; bló-na-˚bab (blo na 'bab) 'I understand' Sch.(?) - 2. mind, thought, memory, čos daṅ yi-ge-la blo jùg-pa (chos dang yi ge la blo 'jug pa) to direct one's thoughts to religion and to learning to read Glr.; bló-la sbyór-ba (blo la sbyor ba) to impress on the mind, to inculcate Glr.; bló-la bžúgs-pa (blo la bzhugs pa) what is retained by, treasured up in the memory Tar.; bló-la bzúṅ-ba (blo la bzung ba) to learn by heart Glr., W.: *loa (*lo a) or ló-na zúm-čè* (lo na zum ce); blo-tág-čód (blo thag chod) v. sub tág-pa (thag pa) I. - 3. mind, sentiment, disposition (here in part = yid (yid)), bló-la ˚dód-pa (blo la 'dod pa) to desire; blo ˚dúṅ-pa (blo 'dung pa) interest, concern, v. ˚dúṅ-pa ('dung pa); mčód-pa byéd-pai bló-čan de (mchod pa byed pa'i blo can de) he that has a mind, is disposed, to sacrifice Dzl.; raṅ bdé-bar ˚dód-pai blo méd-par (rang bde bar 'dod pa'i blo med par) without any regard to his own welfare Thgy.; blo nyé-ba (blo nye ba) friendly sentiment; also: kindly disposed Glr.; sdáṅ-bai blo (sdang ba'i blo) a hating mind, malevolent disposition Lt.; blo gró-ba (blo gro ba) Sch.: 'to get soft, moved, touched, sad', acc. to a native authority: to be agreeably affected by; blo mtún-pa (blo mthun pa) to be of the same mind, like-minded, with supine also: to agree Glr.; perh. also: to be unanimous, peaceable, on friendly terms Sch.; kyéd-kyi blo daṅ mtún-pa (khyed kyi blo dang mthun pa) agreeably to your wish Mil.; blor ma šoṅ (blor ma shong) Sch. 'the mind could not take it in' Tar. 51, 7, Schf.: 'it did not please
